notes: Longview later participated with the Buyer Group, but the chronology also describes Longview as a stockholder advocating for a sale.

consortium collectively referred to as the Buyer Group
notes: Cross-filing evidence identifies the Buyer Group as a consortium comprised of funds advised by BC Partners, La Caisse de dépôt et placement du Québec, affiliates of GIC Special Investments Pte Ltd, affiliates of StepStone Group LP and Longview Asset Management, LLC.

spans.anchor_text: The board also established an ad hoc advisory subcommittee
spans.quote_text: At a meeting on June 18, 2014, the board reviewed, together with a financial advisor and with Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz (“ Wachtell Lipton”), its legal advisor, the various strategic and financial alternatives potentially available to the Company and determined to explore more intensively potential changes to the Company’s capital structure, with a focus on returning capital to stockholders. The board also established an ad hoc advisory subcommittee (the “ad hoc committee”) with respect to,
spans.quote_text_normalized: at a meeting on june 18, 2014, the board reviewed, together with a financial advisor and with wachtell, lipton, rosen & katz (" wachtell lipton"), its legal advisor, the various strategic and financial alternatives potentially available to the company and determined to explore more intensively potential changes to the company's capital structure, with a focus on returning capital to stockholders. the board also established an ad hoc advisory subcommittee (the "ad hoc committee") with respect to,

spans.anchor_text: authorized the ad hoc committee to oversee the sale exploration process
spans.quote_text: for the Company to maximize value for stockholders including by commencing a process to determine the potential value that could be achieved via a sale of the Company; (2) instructed management to commence intensive planning for a cost-reduction and profit-improvement plan (which we refer to collectively as the “ Profit Improvement Plan”) to potentially increase the value of the Company, whether in a sale of the Company or on a standalone basis should no sale materialize; and (3) authorized the ad hoc committee to oversee the sale exploration process, and, in between meetings of the full board, to give direction to the Company’s financial and legal advisors, to lead on behalf of the Company (or to give guidance to the Company’s representatives in connection with) any negotiations with potentially interested parties and periodically to brief the full board on the status of the sale exploration process. Together with the Company’s financial and legal advisors, the board also considered

spans.anchor_text: representatives of J. P. Morgan and Wachtell Lipton
spans.quote_text: On August 13, 2014, the board met in person, together with members of management and representatives of J. P. Morgan and Wachtell Lipton, to complete its initial review of strategic and financial alternatives potentially available to the Company and to receive an update on communications from stockholders and other interested parties, including Industry Participant. As a result of this meeting, the board (1) determined to explore strategic alternatives (including a possible sale of the Company)
spans.quote_text_normalized: on august 13, 2014, the board met in person, together with members of management and representatives of j. p. morgan and wachtell lipton, to complete its initial review of strategic and financial alternatives potentially available to the company and to receive an update on communications from stockholders and other interested parties, including industry participant. as a result of this meeting, the board (1) determined to explore strategic alternatives (including a possible sale of the company)

spans.anchor_text: the Company retained J. P. Morgan as financial advisor
spans.quote_text: things, the review of alternatives. Chairman Gregory Josefowicz and directors Rakesh Gangwal and Thomas Stemberg, all of whom are nonexecutive, independent directors, were appointed to the ad hoc committee. In addition, the board authorized the vetting and retention of a financial advisor to assist in the review. In July, after interviewing several potential financial advisors, the Company retained J. P. Morgan as financial advisor based on, among other factors, J. P. Morgan’s reputation,
spans.quote_text_normalized: things, the review of alternatives. chairman gregory josefowicz and directors rakesh gangwal and thomas stemberg, all of whom are nonexecutive, independent directors, were appointed to the ad hoc committee. in addition, the board authorized the vetting and retention of a financial advisor to assist in the review. in july, after interviewing several potential financial advisors, the company retained j. p. morgan as financial advisor based on, among other factors, j. p. morgan's reputation,

spans.anchor_text: JANA Partners filed a Schedule 13D
spans.quote_text: On July 3, 2014, JANA Partners filed a Schedule 13D with the SEC disclosing that JANA had acquired 9.9% of the Company’s outstanding common stock and that JANA intended to engage in discussions with management and the board with respect to a review of strategic alternatives, including a sale of the Company. On July 7, 2014, Longview made public a letter to the board in which Longview stated that the board should consider the a sale of the Company (as well as other strategic alternatives) and
spans.quote_text_normalized: on july 3, 2014, jana partners filed a schedule 13d with the sec disclosing that jana had acquired 9.9% of the company's outstanding common stock and that jana intended to engage in discussions with management and the board with respect to a review of strategic alternatives, including a sale of the company. on july 7, 2014, longview made public a letter to the board in which longview stated that the board should consider the a sale of the company (as well as other strategic alternatives) and

spans.anchor_text: advocating for a sale of PetSmart
spans.quote_text: board, advocating for a sale of PetSmart. On July 10, 2014, representatives of JANA met in person with representatives of the Company. At this meeting, the JANA representatives presented their perspectives on the Company and advocated for a sale of the Company. On July 10, 2014, representatives of the Company also spoke to representatives of Longview and Longview reiterated its advocacy for a sale of the Company.
spans.quote_text_normalized: board, advocating for a sale of petsmart. on july 10, 2014, representatives of jana met in person with representatives of the company. at this meeting, the jana representatives presented their perspectives on the company and advocated for a sale of the company. on july 10, 2014, representatives of the company also spoke to representatives of longview and longview reiterated its advocacy for a sale of the company.

spans.anchor_text: representatives of Longview met with certain members of the board
spans.quote_text: levered return of capital to stockholders and a sale of the Company. Also during May 2014, representatives of Longview met with certain members of the board and senior management. At those meetings, representatives of Longview presented their views on the business and strategic direction of the Company, but did not advocate for any specific transactions or alternatives.
spans.quote_text_normalized: levered return of capital to stockholders and a sale of the company. also during may 2014, representatives of longview met with certain members of the board and senior management. at those meetings, representatives of longview presented their views on the business and strategic direction of the company, but did not advocate for any specific transactions or alternatives.

spans.anchor_text: Industry Participant might be interested in re-visiting the conversations
spans.quote_text: On August 7, 2014, a representative of Industry Participant contacted a representative of J. P. Morgan. The Industry Participant representative indicated that she had heard rumors that J. P. Morgan was working with the Company and wanted to inform J. P. Morgan that, if the Company were to pursue strategic alternatives, Industry Participant might be interested in re-visiting the conversations that had taken place in the Spring concerning the feasibility of a possible combination of the two
spans.quote_text_normalized: on august 7, 2014, a representative of industry participant contacted a representative of j. p. morgan. the industry participant representative indicated that she had heard rumors that j. p. morgan was working with the company and wanted to inform j. p. morgan that, if the company were to pursue strategic alternatives, industry participant might be interested in re-visiting the conversations that had taken place in the spring concerning the feasibility of a possible combination of the two

spans.anchor_text: Industry Participant would not be invited into the exploratory process
spans.quote_text: with Industry Participant was unlikely to maximize value for PetSmart’s stockholders. The J. P. Morgan representative stated that Industry Participant would not be invited into the exploratory process, but that if Industry Participant were to wish to submit a proposal or other communication to the Company, the board would consider it. There has been no contact between PetSmart and Industry Participant (or between any of their respective representatives) since August 27, 2014.
spans.quote_text_normalized: with industry participant was unlikely to maximize value for petsmart's stockholders. the j. p. morgan representative stated that industry participant would not be invited into the exploratory process, but that if industry participant were to wish to submit a proposal or other communication to the company, the board would consider it. there has been no contact between petsmart and industry participant (or between any of their respective representatives) since august 27, 2014.

spans.anchor_text: 15 potentially interested financial buyers
spans.quote_text: In the first week of October 2014, the Company entered into confidentiality and standstill agreements with 15 potentially interested financial buyers, all 15 of which, during the month of October engaged in due diligence, received management presentations involving the Company’s senior-most management, and received detailed financial and business plan information, including detailed information concerning the Profit Improvement Plan and associated cost-saving plans and opportunities.
spans.quote_text_normalized: in the first week of october 2014, the company entered into confidentiality and standstill agreements with 15 potentially interested financial buyers, all 15 of which, during the month of october engaged in due diligence, received management presentations involving the company's senior-most management, and received detailed financial and business plan information, including detailed information concerning the profit improvement plan and associated cost-saving plans and opportunities.

spans.anchor_text: including the Buyer Group
spans.quote_text: least $80.00 per share, including the Buyer Group, which indicated a range of  $81.00 to $83.00 per share, and another bidder, which suggested a range of  $80.00 to $85.00 per share. As a result of its discussions with J. P. Morgan, another bidder (which we refer to as “ Bidder 2”), which had initially indicated a price of  $78.00, increased its indication to a range of  $81.00 to $84.00 per share.
spans.quote_text_normalized: least $80.00 per share, including the buyer group, which indicated a range of $81.00 to $83.00 per share, and another bidder, which suggested a range of $80.00 to $85.00 per share. as a result of its discussions with j. p. morgan, another bidder (which we refer to as " bidder 2"), which had initially indicated a price of $78.00, increased its indication to a range of $81.00 to $84.00 per share.

spans.anchor_text: the Buyer Group submitted a best and final offer of $83.00 per share
spans.quote_text: During the evening of December 12, 2014, Bidder 2 submitted an offer of  $81.50 per share, in cash. Representatives of J. P. Morgan confirmed via a conversation with Bidder 2 on the evening of December 12, 2014 that $81.50 per share was its best and final offer. The Buyer Group initially submitted an oral offer of $82.50 per share, in cash, but stated that it was working to increase the offer within the next few hours. Later in the evening, the Buyer Group submitted a best and final offer of $83.00 per share, in cash. In addition, each bidder submitted a revised draft of the merger agreement and other transaction agreements, and copies of their respective financing commitment documents. The versions submitted by the Buyer Group were, with few exceptions, in substantially executable form.
spans.quote_text_normalized: during the evening of december 12, 2014, bidder 2 submitted an offer of $81.50 per share, in cash. representatives of j. p. morgan confirmed via a conversation with bidder 2 on the evening of december 12, 2014 that $81.50 per share was its best and final offer. the buyer group initially submitted an oral offer of $82.50 per share, in cash, but stated that it was working to increase the offer within the next few hours. later in the evening, the buyer group submitted a best and final offer of $83.00 per share, in cash. in addition, each bidder submitted a revised draft of the merger agreement and other transaction agreements, and copies of their respective financing commitment documents. the versions submitted by the buyer group were, with few exceptions, in substantially executable form.

spans.quote_text: deliberation and consultation with the Company’s financial and legal advisors, the ad hoc committee authorized these two bidders to work together. We refer to these two bidders together as “ Bidder 3.” Each of the bidders invited into the final round indicated some interest in a Longview rollover, though each also stated that its bid would not be conditioned or dependent on Longview participation.

spans.anchor_text: a verbal indication from Bidder 3
spans.quote_text: On December 10, PetSmart received final bid letters along with revised versions of the merger agreement and other transaction documents from the Buyer Group and from Bidder 2 and a verbal indication from Bidder 3. The Buyer Group offered $80.70 per share, in cash, and Bidder 2 offered $80.35 per share, in cash. Bidder 3 verbally communicated to J. P. Morgan that their valuation would not be above the current stock price of approximately $78 per share. J. P. Morgan communicated to Bidder 3 that

spans.anchor_text: J. P. Morgan was contacted by 27 potential participants
spans.quote_text: During the period from the middle of August through the end of October, the Company developed the Profit Improvement Plan and prepared for a sale process. During this period, J. P. Morgan was contacted by 27 potential participants in a sale process, including three strategic parties (not counting Industry Participant) and 24 financial participants (including potential lead buyers as well as large suppliers of equity capital to lead buyers).

spans.anchor_text: six of the potentially interested parties submitted indications of interest
spans.quote_text: On October 30, six of the potentially interested parties submitted indications of interest. From October 30 to November 2, 2014, representatives of J. P. Morgan spoke by telephone with all of the potentially interested parties, including those that did not submit an indication of interest, to hear the parties’ respective rationales for the price levels suggested in their indications or rationales for not submitting an indication. spans.anchor_text: allow the four bidders
spans.quote_text: On November 3, 2014, the board met in person, together with members of management and representatives of J. P. Morgan and Wachtell Lipton, to continue its review of strategic alternatives, including a review of the indications of interest. The board determined to allow the four bidders that had indicated a price or range at or above $80.00 per share to proceed to the final round of the sale process. Following this meeting, representatives of J. P. Morgan notified the eliminated parties, none of

spans.anchor_text: In March, 2014, the board authorized management to contact Industry Participant
spans.quote_text: levered or unlevered share repurchases or extraordinary dividends, and, from time to time, the Company received inquiries from third parties seeking to determine the Company’s interest in a sale transaction. During the first calendar quarter of 2014, one of the alternatives considered by the board was a merger with or acquisition of another participant in PetSmart’s industry, a privately-held company which we refer to here as “ Industry Participant.” In March, 2014, the board authorized management to contact Industry Participant to determine Industry Participant’s interest in initiating exploratory discussions concerning the feasibility of a merger or acquisition transaction. During the Spring of 2014, David Lenhardt, PetSmart’s chief executive officer, spoke on a few occasions to Industry Participant’s CEO. Industry Participant’s CEO informed Mr. Lenhardt that Industry Participant was not for sale and that, in any event, Industry Participant’s management and owners believed

spans.anchor_text: On August 13, 2014, the board met in person
spans.quote_text: On August 13, 2014, the board met in person, together with members of management and representatives of J. P. Morgan and Wachtell Lipton, to complete its initial review of strategic and financial alternatives potentially available to the Company and to receive an update on communications from stockholders and other interested parties, including Industry Participant. As a result of this meeting, the board (1) determined to explore strategic alternatives (including a possible sale of the Company)

spans.anchor_text: Bidder 2 submitted an offer of  $81.50 per share, in cash
spans.quote_text: During the evening of December 12, 2014, Bidder 2 submitted an offer of  $81.50 per share, in cash. Representatives of J. P. Morgan confirmed via a conversation with Bidder 2 on the evening of December 12, 2014 that $81.50 per share was its best and final offer. The Buyer Group initially submitted an oral offer of $82.50 per share, in cash, but stated that it was working to increase the offer within the next few hours. Later in the evening, the Buyer Group submitted a best and final offer of

spans.anchor_text: submit improved bids on December 12, 2014
spans.quote_text: bidders’ respective best offers, the ad hoc committee instructed J. P. Morgan to inform each bidder that it would need to increase its bid, and to instruct the bidders to submit improved bids on December 12, 2014. On the evening of December 11, 2014, representatives of J. P. Morgan contacted Longview to inform Longview that the bidders have been asked to submit revised and improved bids, and to confirm that Longview continued to be interested and willing to participate in a potential transaction

spans.anchor_text: Longview and the Buyer Group entered into a confidentiality agreement
spans.quote_text: later that day, Longview and the Buyer Group entered into a confidentiality agreement permitting the exchange of detailed information between them, including bid price, which had not previously been shared with Longview. The ad hoc committee also instructed Wachtell Lipton to provide a form of voting agreement to Longview to secure Longview’s commitment to vote for the merger subject to certain exceptions, including a change of recommendation by the board.
spans.quote_text_normalized: later that day, longview and the buyer group entered into a confidentiality agreement permitting the exchange of detailed information between them, including bid price, which had not previously been shared with longview. the ad hoc committee also instructed wachtell lipton to provide a form of voting agreement to longview to secure longview's commitment to vote for the merger subject to certain exceptions, including a change of recommendation by the board.

spans.anchor_text: On December 14, 2014, the parties executed the merger agreement
spans.quote_text: Following the board meeting, the parties completed negotiations on terms satisfactory to both parties, and finalized the terms of the Longview voting agreement. On December 14, 2014, the parties executed the merger agreement, the voting agreement and related transaction agreements and issued a press release announcing the transaction.

spans.anchor_text: On December 14, 2014, PetSmart, Inc. (the “ Company”) entered into an Agreement and Plan of Merger
spans.quote_text: On December 14, 2014, PetSmart, Inc. (the “ Company”) entered into an Agreement and Plan of Merger (the “ Merger Agreement”) with Argos
spans.quote_text_normalized: on december 14, 2014, petsmart, inc. (the " company") entered into an agreement and plan of merger (the " merger agreement") with argos

events.summary: The board authorized management to contact Industry Participant to determine its interest in exploratory merger or acquisition discussions.
PetSmart retained J. P. Morgan as financial advisor after interviewing several potential financial advisors.
notes: The text does not provide a specific day in July.

events.summary: JANA publicly advocated a review of strategic alternatives, including a sale of the Company, in its Schedule 13D filing.
Longview publicly urged the board to consider a sale of the Company.
JANA met with Company representatives and advocated for a sale of the Company.
Longview reiterated to Company representatives its advocacy for a sale of the Company.

date.raw_text: During the weeks following the August 13 board meeting

events.summary: The ad hoc committee directed J. P. Morgan to tell Industry Participant that it would not be invited to participate in the exploratory process.
events.drop_reason_text: The ad hoc committee acted in light of the risks discussed at the August 13 meeting and subsequent discussions.

events.summary: Industry Participant indicated it might be interested in revisiting possible combination discussions if the Company pursued strategic alternatives.
The board determined to explore strategic alternatives, including a possible sale of the Company, and to commence a process to determine potential sale value.
The Company issued a press release announcing that it had determined to explore strategic alternatives, including a possible sale of the Company.
Industry Participant stated that it would have interest in discussing the possibility of a combination of the two companies.
J. P. Morgan told Industry Participant that it would not be invited into the exploratory process, while noting that any proposal or other communication would be considered by the board.
events.drop_reason_text: J. P. Morgan reiterated the board's concerns that engaging with Industry Participant was unlikely to maximize value for PetSmart's stockholders.

events.summary: Potential bidders were informed that non-binding preliminary indications of interest would be due on October 30, 2014.

date.raw_text: In the first week of October 2014

events.summary: The Company entered into confidentiality and standstill agreements with 15 potentially interested financial buyers.
notes: The roster only provides a grouped actor for the 15 financial buyers.

events.summary: Six potentially interested parties submitted indications of interest by the October 30 deadline.
The Buyer Group submitted an indication of interest at $81.00 to $83.00 per share.
events.whole_company_scope_note: The indication was submitted in the sale process for PetSmart.

events.summary: After discussions with J. P. Morgan, Bidder 2 increased its indication from $78.00 per share to $81.00 to $84.00 per share.
notes: The initial $78.00 indication is not separately emitted because the chronology ties it to the same undated submission context and highlights the revised economics after the discussions.
events.whole_company_scope_note: The indication was submitted in the sale process for PetSmart.

events.summary: J. P. Morgan notified the eliminated parties that they were out of the process.
events.drop_reason_text: The eliminated parties had not indicated price levels at or above $80.00 per share.

events.summary: The board decided to allow the four bidders that had indicated a price or range at or above $80.00 per share to proceed to the final round of the sale process.
The Company changed the final bid deadline from December 5 to the evening of December 10, 2014 and asked bidders to submit mark-ups and financing commitments in advance if possible.
The board had initially set December 5, 2014 as the date for submission of bids in the final round.
A confidentiality agreement was executed before introductory meetings involving Longview and the bidders.
notes: The block does not clearly identify all signatories to this confidentiality agreement using rostered actors.

events.summary: Bidder 3 did not submit a written offer after indicating its valuation would not exceed the current stock price and being told it was unlikely to be competitive.
notes: Classified as drop_below_m because the bidder indicated a valuation level below what the process required to be competitive.
events.drop_reason_text: Bidder 3 verbally communicated that its valuation would not be above the current stock price of approximately $78 per share, and J. P. Morgan said it was unlikely to be competitive.

events.summary: Bidder 2 submitted a final bid letter offering $80.35 per share in cash.
events.whole_company_scope_note: Described as a final bid in the sale process with merger agreement drafts and other transaction documents.

events.summary: The Buyer Group submitted a final bid letter offering $80.70 per share in cash.
events.whole_company_scope_note: Described as a final bid in the sale process with merger agreement drafts and other transaction documents.

events.summary: The revised deadline for submission of final bids was the evening of December 10, 2014.
Bidder 2 submitted an offer of $81.50 per share in cash and confirmed it was its best and final offer.
events.whole_company_scope_note: Submitted in the sale process as an offer with revised merger agreement materials.

events.summary: The Buyer Group initially submitted an oral offer of $82.50 per share in cash.
events.whole_company_scope_note: Submitted in the sale process as an acquisition offer.

events.summary: The Buyer Group submitted a best and final offer of $83.00 per share in cash.
events.whole_company_scope_note: Submitted in the sale process as the final acquisition offer.

events.summary: The ad hoc committee required the remaining bidders to submit improved bids on December 12, 2014.
notes: This is the completion point for the post-extension improved bid submission requested after the December 10 review.

events.summary: Longview and the Buyer Group entered into a confidentiality agreement permitting exchange of detailed information, including bid price.
The parties executed the merger agreement and related transaction agreements.
notes: Cross-filing evidence identifies the counterparty consortium as the Buyer Group.

exclusions.explanation: These blocks include background context, board reviews, due diligence, process planning, bidder communications without a taxonomy event, or submission of agreement comments/financing materials without explicit proposal economics.
The statement that there had been no contact since August 27, 2014 is a later status update rather than a separate event.
Another bidder suggested a range of $80.00 to $85.00 per share, but that bidder cannot be confidently linked to an existing actor_id in the locked roster.

Submission and discussion of comments on draft merger agreement, transaction documents, and financing commitment documents did not include explicit proposal economics and therefore are not proposal events.
Committee discussion that bids would need improvement and instructions to seek improved bids are process management context; only the resulting extended deadline is emitted.
Requests and approvals concerning closer work with Longview and potential rollover participation did not themselves constitute a whole-company proposal with explicit economics.
Board consideration of final bids, fairness opinion, and recommendation subject to final documentation resolution are not separate taxonomy events in this schema.

The press release announcing the transaction is mentioned, but sale_press_release and bid_press_release are excluded because the announcement was of an executed transaction rather than a sale process or standalone bid.

unresolved_mentions: Longview is described both as an activist stockholder advocating for a sale and as part of the eventual Buyer Group; the chronology does not clearly re-label Longview as a bidder actor during the process before the final agreement.
The chronology mentions 'another bidder' in B019 that suggested a range of $80.00 to $85.00 per share; it is unclear whether this is one of the two bidders later combined into Bidder 3.
The chronology refers to 'a financial advisor' at the June 18, 2014 meeting without naming that advisor.

The chronology states 15 financial buyers signed confidentiality agreements and six parties submitted indications of interest, but the identities of most bidders other than the Buyer Group, Bidder 2 and Bidder 3 are not disclosed.
another bidder, which suggested a range of $80.00 to $85.00 per share
the unspecified signatories to the confidentiality agreement referenced in B028
extraction_notes: Chunk coverage ends at B026, before receipt of final bids and merger agreement execution in later chronology blocks.
Proposal events were emitted only where explicit per-share economics were stated.
The first-week-of-October confidentiality agreements were linked only to the grouped roster actor for 15 financial buyers because individual counterparties were not identified in the supplied roster.
No event was emitted for B026 because comments on draft merger agreements and financing commitments without explicit economics are excluded under the proposal rules.
Applied prior round context: final_round deadline was Friday, December 5, and final_round_ext completion was the evening of Wednesday, December 10.
Used only rostered actors; no new actors were created for Parent, Merger Sub, or unnamed confidentiality-agreement parties.
Did not emit proposal events from B026 because draft agreement comments and financing commitment documents lacked explicit economics.
